title: 5 ATM Deposits 芒聙聯 Acquirer Procedures
body_text:
5	ATM Deposits 芒聙聯 Acquirer Procedures
5.1	Overview of ATM Deposit Procedures for the Acquirer
Financial institutions that own a full-service ATM must accept deposits via THE EXCHANGE
脗庐
and ACCULINK
脗庐
networks. The ATM owner is the acquirer in the transaction.
ATM deposits can be accepted in Canadian cash, cheques, and other items in Canadian funds drawn on any Canadian financial institution. The acquirer must verify deposits (both cheques and cash), take action on exception items (i.e., notify the issuer and/or initiate an adjustment), and endorse acceptable cheques with an identification stamp.
ATM cash deposits in foreign currency (usually US dollars) must be converted into Canadian funds before depositing the amount to a customer芒聙聶s account. See Section 5.5, ATM 芒聙聯 Verifying Cash and Cheques Received on Deposit.
5.2	Acquirer Liability
In general, the issuer is liable for cheques deposited through THE EXCHANGE and ACCULINK networks. The acquirer may only be liable if it fails in specific duties. See the appropriate network芒聙聶s operating rules for current procedures in full. Some of the highlights are described below.
THE EXCHANGE Transactions
The Canadian Exchange Network Operating Rules stipulate that the acquirer is liable in the following situations:
A deposit meets the conditions for notification and the acquirer doesn芒聙聶t notify the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution.
A deposit is charged back to the acquirer and the acquirer didn芒聙聶t previously endorse the cheque with a stamp indicating that if the cheque is dishonoured, it must be returned to the cardholder芒聙聶s institution.
A deposit is charged back to the acquirer and the acquirer didn芒聙聶t clearly record the name and routing number of the cardholder芒聙聶s institution in the appropriate fields on the stamp.
ACCULINK Transactions
ACCULINK ATM Operating Policies and Procedures stipulate that the acquirer is liable in the following situations:
A deposit meets the conditions specified, and the acquirer doesn芒聙聶t notify the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution.
A deposit is charged back to the acquirer, and the acquirer doesn芒聙聶t notify the issuer by phone or fax within the prescribed time limit.
A deposit is charged back to the acquirer and the acquirer gives notification, but other aspects of the deposit aren芒聙聶t acceptable (e.g., the identification stamp wasn芒聙聶t correctly completed).
5.3	Route and Transit and AFT Adjustment Account Listings
National Route and Transit Listing (ACCULINK Network)
The Canadian Credit Union Association (CCUA) maintains the National Credit Union Route and Transit Listing. This listing also includes the AFT adjustments account for each credit union. Only credit unions that participate in the ACCULINK network are listed in the National Credit Union Route and Transit Listing.
FICANEX Participants (THE EXCHANGE Network)
FICANEX maintains the Canadian Exchange ATM Network Route and Transit Listing. Some of the participants in FICANEX aren芒聙聶t credit unions and are therefore not included on the National Credit Union Route and Transit Listing. FICANEX sends updates of route and transit listings to all FICANEX participants upon request. This document specifies all FICANEX participants and their institution identification numbers. You can also download the list of FICANEX participants from the FICANEX financial institution portal. Obtain login information from FICANEX. See Section 1.6, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources, for contact information.
5.4	Determining the Network Involved
The rules for handling exception deposits are different within THE EXCHANGE and ACCULINK networks. When handling an exception deposit, check the IIN to determine if the transaction is an EXCHANGE deposit or an ACCULINK deposit:
Obtain the Canadian Exchange ATM Network Route and Transit Listing from THE EXCHANGE.
Note:	Check the Canadian Exchange ATM Network Route and Transit Listing first. If the IIN is on this list, the deposit is an EXCHANGE transaction due to priority routing.
Obtain the National Credit Union Route and Transit Listing from the Canadian Credit Union Association (CCUA) website. If the IIN is on this list, the deposit could be an ACCULINK transaction.
5.5	ATM 芒聙聯 Verifying Cash and Cheques Received on Deposit
Verify cash deposited at an ATM by comparing the deposit amount keyed with the value of the cash deposited. If there is a discrepancy, initiate an adjustment. If foreign cash is deposited, convert the cash into Canadian funds and then initiate an adjustment for the difference between the deposit and the Canadian equivalent.
Important
:	All cheques that are accepted for ATM deposit must be photocopied or microfilmed and retained for reference.
Verify cheques deposited at an ATM, using the same criteria as for cheques deposited in person. For example, verify that each cheque
is payable in Canadian funds and drawn on a Canadian financial institution,
isn芒聙聶t post/stale-dated,
has the payee line filled in,
has body and figure numbers that agree,
is signed by the drawer,
isn芒聙聶t double-endorsed (i.e., payable to third party),
isn芒聙聶t payable to acquirer,
isn芒聙聶t payable to cash,
is encoded with route and transit number,
isn芒聙聶t payable in foreign currency,
isn芒聙聶t drawn on a foreign financial institution, and
isn芒聙聶t materially altered regardless of whether it has been initialled.
If a cheque doesn芒聙聶t pass these verifications, request an adjustment for that cheque. If numerous cheques are included in a single deposit, you only need to request an adjustment for a cheque that doesn芒聙聶t meet the criteria. See Chapter 7, ATM Adjustments and Traces.
For THE EXCHANGE deposits, if the deposit amount is $5,000 or more or if it芒聙聶s evident that a cheque has been materially altered (regardless of whether the alteration is initialed), you must notify the card issuer.
5.6	Notifying Issuer of Exception Deposits
Notify the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution immediately by phone or confirmed facsimile in the situations specified below. Consult the appropriate route and transit listing for the phone number and fax number of the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution. See Section 5.3, Route and Transit and AFT Adjustment Account Listings.
This notification allows the issuer to hold funds on the deposit if the issuer chooses to do so. If you fail to provide this notification, your financial institution will be held responsible for any losses incurred.
You must send a notification either before the close of business on the date of the deposit or the next business day that the issuer is open. We recommend that you send a fax if the issuer isn芒聙聶t open the next business day that your financial institution is open.
If the situation also requires an adjustment, follow the regular procedure for obtaining an adjustment. See Chapter 7, ATM Adjustments and Traces. Include the name of the person you spoke to at the issuer芒聙聶s office (if you phoned), and the date and time of your phone call or fax on the adjustment form.
THE EXCHANGE Deposit Notification Requirements
The Canadian Exchange Network Operating Rules stipulate that the acquirer must notify the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution as follows:
If the deposit value is $5,000 or greater, fax
芒聢聮
a copy of the front and back of the singular cheque that is valued at $5,000 or greater,
芒聢聮
a confirmation of a cash deposit greater than $5,000, if applicable, and
芒聢聮
a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If the deposit value is $10,000 or greater, fax
芒聢聮
a copy of the front and back of all cheques that make up all or part of the deposit,
芒聢聮
a confirmation of a cash deposit greater than $5,000, if applicable, and
芒聢聮
a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If it芒聙聶s evident that a cheque has been materially altered, regardless of whether the alteration is initialed, fax
芒聢聮
a copy of the front and back of the cheque, and
芒聢聮
a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If a debit or credit adjustment to a deposit is required because the difference is $20.01 or greater, fax a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If an empty deposit envelope is received, fax a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
ACCULINK Transaction Notification Requirements
ACCULINK ATM Operating Policies and Procedures stipulate that the acquirer must notify the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution if:
A deposit contains an error of $20.01 or greater.
An empty deposit envelope is received.
The deposit value is $10,000 or greater.
Note:	Fax a copy of the front and back of all cheques that make up all or part of the deposit.
A cheque isn芒聙聶t encoded with the route and transit numbers.
5.7	Identification Stamps
In an EXCHANGE or ACCULINK deposit, the acquirer is acting as an agent for the issuing financial institution. The acquirer must stamp deposited cheques using a manual or mechanical identification stamp that meets CPA Standard 006 and CPA Rule A3.
Note:	Appendix II of CPA Rule A3, Endorsement and Identification of Paper Items, provides examples of mechanical and manual ATM identification stamps for reference purposes only.
Stamp cheques that have passed your verification. Don芒聙聶t stamp a cheque if you need to adjust the deposit and send the cheque back to the issuer.
Ensure your stamp is legible.
Ensure you enter the issuer芒聙聶s route and transit in the 芒聙聹if dishonoured芒聙聺 portion. Failure to do so may affect your recourse in case of a chargeback. See Section 5.8, Chargebacks (Acquirer).
See Figure 1 and Figure 2 below for completed examples of a manual identification stamp and a mechanical or electronic identification stamp.
Figure 1: Completed Manual Identification Stamp
Figure 2: Completed Mechanical/Electronic Identification Stamp
Incorrectly or Illegibly Stamped Cheques
Central 1 isn芒聙聶t responsible for tracing and/or routing a cheque that the acquirer didn芒聙聶t stamp correctly and legibly. If the stamp is incorrect or illegible,
the cheque will be routed to the acquirer if it芒聙聶s returned through the clearing system, and
the acquirer will be responsible for any loss incurred due to a delay in processing.
5.8	Chargebacks (Acquirer)
The Canadian Exchange Network Operating Rules and the ACCULINK ATM Operating Policies and Procedures stipulate that if a deposit is charged back to your financial institution, you must notify the issuer by phone or confirmed facsimile, either
before the close of business on the day you received notice of the chargeback, or
the next business day that the issuer is open.
We recommend that you send a confirmed fax if the issuer isn芒聙聶t open the next business day that your financial institution is open.
Consult the appropriate route and transit listing for the phone and fax numbers of the cardholder芒聙聶s financial institution. See Section 5.3, Route and Transit and AFT Adjustment Account Listings.
The acquirer will be liable for the cheque if the acquirer
hasn芒聙聶t provided notification of the chargeback, or
has provided notification, but other aspects of the deposit aren芒聙聶t acceptable (e.g., the identification stamp isn芒聙聶t correctly completed).
5.9	Bill Payments (Acquirer)
If a customer enters a bill payment correctly as a 芒聙聹payment,芒聙聺 you can either remit the payment directly to the vendor or forward the payment and stub to the issuer for processing.
If a customer enters a bill payment incorrectly as a 芒聙聹deposit,芒聙聺 you must initiate an adjustment with the issuer for the entire amount of the payment. See Chapter 7, ATM Adjustments and Traces.
END
